mainEntity.name: I was partially at fault in my Boise construction injury—can I still get money?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes. Workers' compensation is a no-fault system in Idaho, so you are eligible for benefits even if you caused the accident, provided you weren't intoxicated or acting intentionally.
acceptedAnswer.description: Yes, you can still receive benefits even if you were partially at fault for your construction accident in Boise or anywhere in Idaho. Workers' compensation is a 'no-fault' system, meaning your negligence—such as tripping or making a mistake—usually doesn't disqualify you from receiving medical coverage and wage replacement. The only major exceptions are if the injury was intentional or caused by intoxication. However, if you are pursuing a third-party lawsuit against a manufacturer, Idaho's modified comparative negligence rules may reduce your award if you are found more than 50% at fault. An attorney ensures the insurance company doesn't unfairly blame you to deny the claim.
